lanes.fast-local: Host-only checks for every build or small patch.
lanes.heavy-backend: Longer backend compatibility and runtime regressions.
lanes.heavy-container: Container/rootfs probes that need a prepared rootfs.
lanes.android-service-truth: Focused Android planned-gap service truth evidence.
lanes.android-gpu: Android GPU executor and bridge measurements.
lanes.governance: Strict governance gates that may be known-failing and non-promoting until closure criteria are met.
lanes.android-runtime-teardown: Focused Android runtime teardown proof/reducer evidence.

scenarios.checks: External contract stability, bug fossilization guard, Engine API golden shape, Compose/Dockerfile fixtures, abnormal replay, planned archive/state/artifact gaps.
Abnormal event schema, required failure categories, reproduction commands, evidence sources, planned gaps, and release-auditable artifact shape.
Engine API route inventory, Docker-compatible endpoints, static protocol surface, and backend quick hooks.
Runtime state, pid reconciliation, process lifecycle contracts, and backend invariants.
Direct syscall hook inventory, scenario manifest integrity, path variants, boundary values, branch decisions, local cases, and Android dry-run plan.
Container-root shell basics, long argv preservation, proc view, and large allocation guard behavior.
Standard Dockerfile compatibility stance, unsupported BuildKit feature handling, template syntax contracts, and pdockerd Dockerfile COPY glob semantics against bundled project contexts.
Engine-native compose up path, build log streaming, container labels, service URLs, job state, and retry/stop actions.
Terminal PTY UI, selection/copy, keyboard suppression, readonly log terminals, editor line numbers, whitespace, and pinch zoom.

Engine exec -it path, PTY fallback, Enter, Ctrl-C, ArrowUp/history, top refresh/q, resize, IME fallback, host artifact verifier, bracket argv regression, and Android smoke wiring. Promotion requires ui-it-selftest JSON plus raw engine-exec-input JSONL verified with --require-container; skipped/static checks are non-promoting.
scenarios.checkpoint_credit: device-only; non-promoting without raw Engine exec JSONL and require-container verification

scenarios.checks: Bundled dev workspace, direct runtime probe, pdocker test-suite exec container, llama, ROS2, Blender templates, shared Documents volumes, healthchecks, and launch metadata.

Static template contract for code-server, Continue, Codex, Claude Code hooks, pdocker management helpers, paths, and default workspace repair/migration; paired device hard gate requires compose/build/run, current skydnir-dev Engine state, 18080 listener, code-server HTTP 2xx/3xx, configured extension evidence, and UI card truth agreement.
SAF mediator writes nested exports, evicts app-private mirror payloads after success, and preserves Unix-like sidecar metadata.
Unique/shared layer accounting, image/container/cache buckets, fixture/capture contract, and the baseline/build/rebuild/edit/prune sequence artifact.
Layer cache reuse, prune behavior, active build protection, and storage refresh wiring.

App-code-backed UI contract for storage/layer maintenance: MainActivity renderImages, renderImageCacheHealth/imageCacheHealth, renderImageReferenceTree/imageParentMap, ImageGraphLayout connector drawing, imageDetail/imageReferenceInfos, and ImageGraphAction now provide distinct image/cache rows, unique/shared/stale size evidence, tree details/files/delete+clean-cache actions, and stale build-cache or unreferenced-layer garbage surfacing.
scenarios.checkpoint_credit: app-code evidence plus host static verifier; release credit still requires a future device screenshot/manual visual artifact for continuous connectors and tap actions

scenarios.checks: Host source and synthetic-runner gate for atomic image pull publication: .pull/.old image staging, tmp layer staging, digest verification, startup prune/recovery, partial-image rejection, and non-promoting device/live-pull evidence shape.
scenarios.checkpoint_credit: fast local regression gate only; release credit still requires image.pull.live-registry-interruption device evidence

scenarios.checks: Timed live registry pull interruption plan for a scenario-owned or isolated fixture. It must prove daemon kill/restart during transfer never publishes partial tags/layers or overwrites user images; current plan artifacts are non-promoting until the live device phase is implemented and passes.
scenarios.checkpoint_credit: non-promoting planned-gap until live_interrupted_network_pull=true with scenario-owned evidence

scenarios.checks: COW copy-up, path metadata, fd lifecycle, xattr hooks, JSON microbenchmarks, hardlink ring cache rebuild, and kill-at-step planned-gap evidence.

Device daemon/helper kill-at-step route for copy-up, rename, whiteout, archive PUT, metadata, low-space, startup restart, and scoped cleanup. Planned-gap or blocked-device artifacts are non-promoting until every required case is proven by adb/run-as evidence.
scenarios.checkpoint_credit: device-only; non-promoting unless status=pass and promotion condition is satisfied

scenarios.checks: Host archive API gate for GET/PUT/HEAD container archive routes, tar/stat header compatibility, path traversal defense, whiteout/lower-upper merge behavior, and pdockerd asset mirror freshness. Host-only regression evidence is non-promoting for storage crash-safety release credit until device COW/archive mutation gates pass.
Host-like network metadata, port mapping truth states, service aliases, warnings, and UI/docker ps consistency.
Vulkan ICD bridge dispatch, executor socket handshake, conservative feature advertisement, and bridge smoke path.
Host CPU/GPU baseline, Vulkan/GLES executor measurements, and device-independent GPU executor evidence.
Host/container bridge overhead, repeated GPU bridge measurements, and comparison artifact update.
llama.cpp CPU fallback benchmark, repeatability, health endpoint, and JSON benchmark artifact.
Container llama.cpp GPU path without modifying llama.cpp, CPU baseline reuse, target speedup, blocker classification, and UI operation reporting.
Audio/video API proxy boundary, descriptor capability contract, and no raw /dev passthrough claim.
SDK28-compatible virtual memory pager design, mmap/mprotect/signal assumptions, and documented device PoC boundaries.
Project/container/image identity, metadata rebuild strategy, corruption recovery, and Git/project relationship rules.
Requirement-level blackbox scenarios, observable surfaces, positive/negative oracles, and self-tests checking fail-expected cases actually fail.
API argument validation, malformed JSON rejection, BNF-like input grammar coverage, explicit Compose full-grammar planned gap, numeric range checks, and boundary coverage obligations.
Seeded random API fuzzing, monkey/stress scenario ledger, build-set artifact schema, and repeat variance detection.
Currently failing 2x literal token gate, required test design axes, Python coverage artifact, governance runner wiring, per-change test design policy, and build-set artifact rules.
scenarios.reason: The strict literal check-count to implementation-code token ratio is recorded but not yet high enough to be a passing fast lane.
acceptance_scope: literal token ratio reaches the configured 2.0x minimum without lowering the standard
C0/C1/C2 structural evidence and instrumented coverage remain recorded as evidence
the governance lane writes the artifact and exits zero
exit_criteria: python3 scripts/verify-test-design-criteria.py --write-artifact docs/test/test-design-criteria-latest.json exits zero
docs/test/test-design-criteria-latest.json reports literal_check_count_at_2x_token_target_met=true

scenarios.checks: Mutation testing planned gap: condition inversion, error-path deletion, boundary mutation, path rewrite mutation, and storage accounting mutation must be killed by existing tests before release claim.
scenarios.reason: Mutation engine and mutation corpus are not implemented yet.
exit_criteria: All listed mutation operators have runnable cases
Mutation survivors are recorded as issues or planned gaps
Mutation artifact is tied to git commit and build flavor

scenarios.checks: Property-based testing planned gap: generated API JSON, Dockerfile syntax, Compose environment expansion, path rewrite invariants, and storage accounting invariants.
scenarios.reason: Generated input strategies are not implemented yet.
exit_criteria: Generators are seed-fixed
Shrunk failing examples are recorded
Properties run in fast or heavy lanes with artifacts

scenarios.checks: Differential testing planned gap: compare Docker-compatible API shapes, Dockerfile parsing, Compose env expansion, tar/archive behavior, and command output against upstream Docker fixtures.
scenarios.reason: Upstream Docker fixture runner is not implemented yet.
exit_criteria: Golden upstream outputs are versioned
Differences are classified as compatible, pdocker-specific, or bug
Regression command is documented

scenarios.checks: Stateful model testing planned gap: image, container, layer, project, job, daemon operation, card, and docker ps state transitions must preserve model invariants.
scenarios.reason: State machine model runner is not implemented yet.
acceptance_scope: UI card and docker ps consistency
exit_criteria: Model invariants are executable
Generated operation sequences are reproducible
Mismatches produce minimal traces

scenarios.checks: Concurrency/race testing planned gap: compose up while stop, retry, log follow, UI restart, daemon reconnect, Documents sync, and docker ps refresh happen concurrently.
scenarios.reason: Concurrent operation harness is not implemented yet.
acceptance_scope: Documents sync during docker ps refresh
exit_criteria: Race schedule is seed-fixed
No ANR or stale card state
Final engine snapshot matches UI summary

scenarios.checks: Crash/recovery testing planned gap: daemon kill, UI kill, process residue, interrupted build, interrupted pull, interrupted Documents sync, and stale job replay must recover with truthful state.
scenarios.reason: Crash injection and recovery harness is not implemented yet.
exit_criteria: Recovery leaves truthful engine state
Logs survive or record loss reason
No orphan process leaks remain

scenarios.checks: Fault injection planned gap: ENOSPC, EACCES, OOM-near allocation, socket timeout, partial write, corrupt metadata, broken layer, and unavailable GPU/media executor failures.
scenarios.reason: Fault injection adapters are not implemented yet.
exit_criteria: Each fault has expected error class
No fault returns fake success
Recovery or cleanup evidence is recorded

scenarios.checks: Golden compatibility corpus planned gap: fixed Dockerfiles, compose files, Engine API requests/responses, tar archives, docker ps outputs, and known external project templates.
scenarios.reason: Golden corpus runner and fixture set are not implemented yet.
exit_criteria: Fixtures are versioned
Expected outputs are stable
Intentional pdocker differences are documented

scenarios.checks: Security/adversarial testing planned gap: path traversal, symlink escape, tar bomb, zip slip, malicious Dockerfile, huge JSON, long argv, unsafe archive paths, and SAF mediator escape attempts.
scenarios.reason: Adversarial payload corpus is not implemented yet.
exit_criteria: Each payload has a failure oracle
No payload escapes declared roots
Large input limits are documented

scenarios.checks: Performance regression gates planned gap: ptrace, COW, build, apt, GPU bridge, llama, UI log rendering, and storage scans must track p50/p95/p99, variance, and previous-artifact deltas.
scenarios.reason: Thresholded performance regression gate is not implemented yet.
exit_criteria: Baseline artifact is versioned
Thresholds are explicit
Regression result is pass, fail, or recorded blocker

scenarios.checks: Focused Android docker run equivalent for ubuntu:22.04 echo hi with strict artifact verification: exact stdout hi, exit code 0, real 64-hex Engine container ID from docker --cidfile, linked device stdout/stderr/log/cidfile evidence, and no host-shell fallback.

Executable acceptance plan for service truth: UI card, Engine API /containers/json, persisted state.json, process table, listener probe, and logs must all agree on the same current Engine container ID before healthy/running is allowed.
negative_cases: configured port without listener stays unhealthy
stale listener from a different PID/container stays unhealthy
stale exited duplicate name cannot satisfy the card
successful compose/build job metadata alone stays insufficient
logs from a previous Engine container ID are rejected
exit_criteria: Evidence artifact records package, device, build, timestamp, service name, port, Engine container ID, PID, and log marker.
UI card container ID equals Engine API ID, persisted state ID, process-table owner, listener owner, and log owner for 18080 and 18081.
Any mismatch among UI, Engine API, state.json, process table, listener probe, or logs reports unhealthy/not-running and exits nonzero.
Duplicate names are display-only; project/service labels plus Engine container ID are the relationship truth.

scenarios.checks: npm install -g npm@latest compatibility, rename/reify path behavior, symlink replacement, long argv preservation, and errno parity.
SAF Phase 2 delete propagation, rename detection, conflict quarantine, rescan UX, and Unix metadata emulation limits.
SAF/SD/FAT UnixFS backend contract, sidecar metadata, capability reporting, failure recovery, and proof that overlay/archive/runtime/UI use backend abstractions instead of Android storage internals.
Actual port forwarding/proxy, active listener evidence, requested versus active mapping, conflict handling, and future syscall-hook port rewrite integration.

Executable acceptance plan for runtime teardown: stop/kill/rm is accepted only when Engine state, persisted state, process table, lifecycle logs, and duplicate-name cleanup prove the current Engine container ID has no live process-tree residue.
negative_cases: HTTP 204 stop response with live pdocker-direct child is failure
stale PID in state.json is rejected after process death or PID reuse
GPU executor or service child process orphan after stop is failure
stale exited duplicate name cannot hide the current running container
exit_criteria: Stop/kill/rm evidence records the Engine container ID before and after teardown, API status, persisted state, process-tree snapshot, and lifecycle log marker.
After stop, Engine API and state.json show exited/removed for that same ID and no pdocker-direct, service child, listener, or GPU executor process remains for it.
A 204/success API acknowledgement without process-tree teardown exits nonzero.
Restart after teardown creates a fresh Engine container ID or explicitly reconciles the previous ID without duplicate-name ambiguity.

scenarios.checks: Runtime memory telemetry ring, early ENOMEM guard event, LMK-suspected restart classification, Large Workload Mode metadata, opt-in managed/bridge memory paths, and an explicit non-promoting device replay plan until controlled LMK/backend-death evidence passes.
scenarios.checkpoint_credit: non-promoting planned-gap until connected-device replay artifact passes

scenarios.checks: Host-only signing material exclusion, secret scan, payload inventory, F-Droid readiness language, publication safety, and checkpoint-honesty wording. This is non-promoting: planned-gap or device-gated blockers remain release/stable blockers until their device artifacts pass the CI gate ledger promotion conditions.
Installed APK device smoke, daemon lifecycle, Engine API actions, compose route, terminal/exec path, and no orphan cleanup regressions.

scenarios.command: future focused adb/run-as pdocker-direct linkat hardlink probe
scenarios.checks: Android linkat hardlink semantics must prove same st_dev/st_ino, st_nlink growth/decrement, write-through from either name, Linux errno parity, and restart recovery; the current copy fallback is non-promoting.
scenarios.reason: Current direct-runtime linkat emulation can fall back to byte-copy behavior and cannot promote hardlink identity without device proof.
exit_criteria: Device artifact reports status=pass and success=true
Copy fallback evidence remains fail-closed/non-promoting

scenarios.checks: Docker CLI docker cp promotion requires same Engine container ID, archive HEAD/GET/PUT, byte/sha256 equality, hardlink/symlink and metadata policy, xattr, whiteout rejection, and escape-negative cases.
scenarios.reason: Host archive API coverage is non-promoting for device CLI copy parity.
exit_criteria: Device artifact reports Status=pass and Success=true
Host-only or name-only artifacts remain non-promoting

scenarios.checks: Modern/no-PRoot flavor must either execute containers for real or hide/disable execution paths with structured capability errors; metadata-only routes must not claim RUN/docker run/Compose/service success.
scenarios.reason: CI ledger tracks this P0 blocker and the feature scenario ledger must own the device proof.
exit_criteria: Modern-flavor artifact proves fail-closed behavior or real executor support
Metadata-only success claims are absent

scenarios.checks: Host gate for Dockerfile RUN changed-path manifests: wildcard chmod paths expand against rootfs, literal absolute paths stay scoped, and unsafe relative/env/unmatched/non-chmod commands fall back to full snapshots instead of recording false narrow layers.
scenarios.checkpoint_credit: fast parser regression gate only; release performance credit still requires device build-profile evidence for broad snapshot avoidance

scenarios.checks: Kotlin build context tar writer must cover regular files, directories, symlinks, executable modes, long paths/PAX behavior, mtimes, and .dockerignore parity.
scenarios.reason: Host source/compile contract is implemented; connected-device byte corpus and upstream Docker differential parity remain non-promoting release evidence.
exit_criteria: Verifier rejects metadata-losing tar output
Bundled and external context fixtures pass
